summary: A test in package github.com/cli/cli/v2/internal/prompter timed out: form.Run() did not complete before the test's timeout, causing the suite to fail.
root_cause: The test TestHuhPrompterMultiSelectWithSearchPersistence (subtest selections_persist_after_changing_search_query) failed because form.Run() did not complete in time, causing the test to time out.

evidence.reason: Shows the top-level test that failed and that it timed out after ~5.19s.
Identifies the specific failing subtest and its duration.

evidence.quote: huh_prompter_test.go:478: form.Run() did not complete in time
evidence.reason: Concrete failure message from the test indicating form.Run() blocked or did not finish before the test timeout.
Indicates the package test run failed, causing the overall CI job to exit non-zero.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the failing test locally and investigate why form.Run() blocks (e.g. run: go test -v ./internal/prompter -run TestHuhPrompterMultiSelectWithSearchPersistence). Add logging or adjust mocks/inputs so form.Run() can complete, or increase the test timeout if the operation legitimately needs more time.

summary: The test suite failed because a PubSub test timed out waiting to receive a message on a channel.
root_cause: A test assertion timed out: the PubSub spec expecting to receive a message did not receive anything within the 1s timeout.

evidence.reason: Shows the failing test and the test file path where it failed.

evidence.quote:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1.001s.
  Expected
      <<-chan *redis.Message | len:0, cap:10>: 0xc0012d96c0
  to receive something.
evidence.reason: The test failure message explicitly shows a timeout and that the channel was expected to receive a message but did not.
Confirms there was exactly one failing spec and points to the lines in the test file.

evidence.quote:
FAIL	github.com/redis/go-redis/v9	185.245s
make: *** [Makefile:46: test.ci] Error 1
##[error]Process completed with exit code 2.
evidence.reason: Shows the overall test run failed and caused the CI job to exit with an error code.

suggested_fix: Run the failing spec locally and inspect pubsub_test.go around the reported lines to determine why the subscription channel never received a message. For example, reproduce with the test runner used in CI: cd /home/runner/work/go-redis/go-redis && go test -v ./... -run 'PubSub' (or run the specific ginkgo spec) and then (1) verify Redis/test fixtures are up and publishing in the test setup, (2) check the test timeout and increase it if the environment is slow, and (3) add logging or assertions inside pubsub_test.go near lines 645–661 to trace subscription/publish operations.

summary: C++ compilation failed while building pandas SIMD code: explicit template instantiation for accumulate_moments_simd::operator() used xsimd::neon64 but the declared template signature uses xsimd::neon, causing the build to stop.
root_cause: The compiler reports explicit instantiation errors: an instantiation with xsimd::neon64 does not match the declared candidate template which uses xsimd::neon (neon vs neon64 mismatch) in the SIMD moments code, causing clang++ to fail compilation.

evidence.quote: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Shows the compiler emitted an explicit-instantiation error that caused the compile to fail.
The code attempts an explicit instantiation with xsimd::neon64 (the instantiation site).

evidence.quote: candidate template ignored: could not match 'Moments (xsimd::neon64, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int) noexcept' ... against 'Moments (xsimd::neon, std::span<const double>, int, std::optional<std::span<const uint8_t>>, int)'
evidence.reason: Compiler note shows the declared candidate template uses xsimd::neon while the instantiation used xsimd::neon64, indicating a type/name mismatch.
Build target failed (compilation error) for the neon SIMD instantiation object file.
Confirms the build system stopped because the compiler subcommand failed.

suggested_fix: Make the template declaration and explicit instantiation use the same xsimd SIMD type. Concretely, update the code in ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp or ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp so the extern template / template instantiation targets the same type (either xsimd::neon or xsimd::neon64) for this platform, or add conditional compilation to select the correct xsimd type for the macOS aarch64 build, then rebuild.

summary: The pnpm audit step found a moderate vulnerability in the ip-address package (<=10.1.0) and the audit command exited non‑zero, causing the job to fail.
root_cause: pnpm audit reported 1 moderate vulnerability in the transitive dependency ip-address (vulnerable versions <=10.1.0; patched in >=10.1.1) and exited with a failure code.

evidence.quote: moderate            │ ip-address has XSS in Address6 HTML-emitting methods
evidence.reason: Shows the vulnerability and the affected package ip-address.
Specifies the vulnerable version range for ip-address.
Shows which versions contain the fix (actionable upgrade target).
Shows the dependency path by which ip-address is pulled into the project (transitive dependency chain).
Confirms audit detected one vulnerability.
Indicates the severity level reported by the audit.
Shows the audit command returned a non-zero exit code, causing the job to fail.
Shows pnpm-lock.yaml is present and relevant to dependency state (lockfile backing the audit).

suggested_fix: Upgrade the transitive dependency to a patched ip-address version (>=10.1.1) — e.g. update the package(s) that depend on ip-address (socks, socks-proxy-agent or their parents), then regenerate the lockfile (run pnpm install to update pnpm-lock.yaml) and re-run pnpm audit. Alternatively, run an appropriate audit command (e.g. pnpm audit fix) if available after updating dependencies.

summary: Jest snapshot tests failed because parser error messages changed (dynamic import / TypeScript parse errors), causing snapshot mismatches in multiple test suites.
root_cause: Snapshot mismatches due to changed parser/parse error messages (e.g. for `import()` and TypeScript constructor/declare errors) — the received error text no longer matches stored snapshots.

evidence.reason: Shows a failing test suite (snapshot tests under js/_errors_/import).
The stored snapshot expected an "Unexpected token" message.

evidence.quote: + `import()` requires exactly one or two arguments. (1:1)
evidence.reason: The actual received error message is different (`import()` requires exactly one or two arguments), which caused the snapshot mismatch.

evidence.quote: SyntaxError: A parameter initializer is only allowed in a function or constructor implementation. (3:17)
evidence.reason: A TypeScript parsing error appears in the failing TypeScript test, indicating parser-produced error text that may differ from snapshot expectations.
Stack shows the error originates from the Babel parse error creation path, implicating parser error-message output as the source of changed text.

evidence.quote:
Snapshot Summary
 › 13 snapshots failed from 2 test suites. Inspect your code changes or re-run jest with `-u` to update them.
evidence.reason: Jest reports snapshot failures and suggests re-running jest with -u to update snapshots, confirming the nature of the failure.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 3 failed, 1500 passed, 1503 total
Tests:       14 failed, 5 skipped, 29634 passed, 29653 total
Snapshots:   13 failed, 12086 passed, 12099 total
evidence.reason: Quantifies the failure as multiple snapshot/test failures among many passing tests — indicating a localized but concrete regression in error text/output.

suggested_fix: If the changed error messages are intentional, update the snapshots (per the log suggestion) by re-running tests with snapshot update, e.g. `yarn test -u` or `jest -u`. If the change is not intentional, inspect recent changes to the JS/TS parser (see src/language-js/parse/babel.js and create-babel-parse-error.js in the stack) and revert or adjust the parser so error messages match the expected snapshots, then re-run tests.
